coredata.dc:title: Facile preparation of bimetallic Au-Cu nanoclusters as fluorescent nanoprobes for sensitive detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions
coredata.prism:publicationName: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy
coredata.dc:creator: Shi, Zhiying
Hu, Bangyang
Ge, Shengya
Chi, Baozhu
Yan, Xiluan
Zheng, Xiangjuan
coredata.dc:description: Metallic nanoclusters (NCs) have attracted special attention from researchers due to their interesting optical properties. In this experiment, we proposed a facile one-step method for the synthesis of bimetallic gold-copper nanoclusters (AuCuNCs). The prepared AuCuNCs were characterized by fluorescence spectroscopy (FL), UV–vis absorption spectrum, transmission electron microscopy (TEM), etc. The emission peak of the prepared AuCuNCs was located at 455 nm and showed blue luminescence under the excitation of 365 nm UV light. Furthermore, after the addition of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions, the FL emission intensity of AuCuNCs was significantly reduced at 455 nm and there was a color change of diminished blue luminescence under UV lamp. The AuCuNCs exhibited excellent linearity and sensitivity for the detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions. The limits of detection (LOD) for the Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions were calculated to be 1.5 and 0.037 μM, respectively. Finally, the recoveries of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions in Runxi Lake and tap water were measured by standard addition recovery test and were 96.66 ∼ 116.29 %, 95.75 ∼ 119.4 %.
coredata.dcterms:subject: AuCuNCs
Fluorescence detection
Cr3+
S2O8 2

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Determination of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions by AuCuNCs
Fluorescence detection of AuCuNCs nanoprobes in real samples
Determination of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions by AuCuNCs
Response of AuCuNCs to different anions and cations
Fluorescence detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions in real samples

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: TEM image of AuCuNCs (A). Particle size distribution map of AuCuNCs (B). EDX image of AuCuNCs (C) and AuCuNCs background (D).

Fluorescence emission spectra recorded at diverse excitation wavelengths for AuCuNCs (A). Comparison of FL emission spectra and UV–vis spectra of AuCuNCs (B). The inset shows the color comparison of AuCuNCs under sunlight (left) and UV lamps (right).
Feasibility analysis of Cr3+/S2O8 2- ions and AuCuNCs experiments (λex = 370 nm). The inset shows the corresponding photos under UV light.

FL emission plots of AuCuNCs nanoprobes with different concentrations of Cr3+ (A) and S2O8 2- (C) at an excitation wavelength of 370 nm. The relationships between the Cr3+ concentration (B), S2O8 2- concentration(D) and the (F0 - F) / F0 value of AuCuNCs, respectively. And illustrations are linear plots at the concentration of 0 to 4000 μM Cr3+ and the concentration of 0 to 40 μM S2O8 2-. F is the FL intensity of AuCuNCs after adding Cr3+ or S2O8 2- ions. F0 is the FL intensity of blank AuCuNCs.
Fluorescence lifetime decay curves of AuCuNCs and AuCuNCs after the addition of 5 mM Cr3+ or 0.5 mM S2O8 2- ions.
Preparation of AuCuNCs nanomaterials and the process of Cr3+/S2O8 2- ions detection.
Performance analysis of AuCuNCs probes for Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions detection and comparison with other methods.

head.ce:title: Facile preparation of bimetallic Au-Cu nanoclusters as fluorescent nanoprobes for sensitive detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Nanchang University, Nanchang 330031, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Nanchang University, Nanchang 330031, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Pharmacy, Nanchang University, Nanchang 330031, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Pharmacy, Nanchang University, Nanchang 330031, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A novel fluorescent nanoprobe based on Au-Cu bimetallic nanoclusters for sensitive detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions. Blue-emitting AuCuNCs can be synthesized by a simple and green method, which has good fluorescence stability and water solubility.

Metallic nanoclusters (NCs) have attracted special attention from researchers due to their interesting optical properties. In this experiment, we proposed a facile one-step method for the synthesis of bimetallic gold-copper nanoclusters (AuCuNCs). The prepared AuCuNCs were characterized by fluorescence spectroscopy (FL), UV–vis absorption spectrum, transmission electron microscopy (TEM), etc. The emission peak of the prepared AuCuNCs was located at 455 nm and showed blue luminescence under the excitation of 365 nm UV light. Furthermore, after the addition of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions, the FL emission intensity of AuCuNCs was significantly reduced at 455 nm and there was a color change of diminished blue luminescence under UV lamp. The AuCuNCs exhibited excellent linearity and sensitivity for the detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions. The limits of detection (LOD) for the Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions were calculated to be 1.5 and 0.037 μM, respectively. Finally, the recoveries of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions in Runxi Lake and tap water were measured by standard addition recovery test and were 96.66 ∼ 116.29 %, 95.75 ∼ 119.4 %.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: No data was used for the research described in the article.

ce:section.ce:para: Metal ions released as waste during industrial processes can be introduced into river water, posing a serious threat to human life and health and the living environment [1]. Therefore, the release of these substances should be regulated. As a kind of metal ion, chromium(III) (Cr3+) is an essential trace element for human body. It maintains the balance of trace elements in the body and plays an important role in regulating various biological events [2]. The Cr3+ deficiency may lead to different health disorders including nervous system disorders, diabetes and cardiovascular diseases [3]. However, high concentrations of Cr3+ can bind to DNA, negatively impacting cell structure and damaging cellular components, which is also harmful to human health [4]. Therefore, the detection of Cr3+ is of great significance for environmental protection and human health. Persulfate (S2O8 2-) is a powerful oxidant with a wide range of uses. For example, the degradation of carbamazepine was examined by Fe(II)-activated persulfate process [5]. And the disinfection effect of persulfate system in seawater was studied [6]. According to previous reports, S2O8 2- had been found to be the cause of occupational asthma and skin diseases [7,8]. Therefore, with the development of the application of persulfate salt, it is very necessary to detect S2O8 2-.

Fluorescence sensors are popular in trace substances detection because of their high sensitivity, cost-effectiveness and simplicity [9]. Thanks to the efforts of researchers, various sensing materials have been applied in many fluorescence sensing fields, such as carbon dots (CDs) [10], quantum dots (QDs) [11], metal nanoclusters (MNCs) [12], etc . Regarding MNCs, there are corresponding literature reports on fluorescence detection. For example, the disulfide bond-functionalized gold nanoclusters (S-S-AuNCs) as fluorescent sensors to detect organophosphorus pesticides [13], Tb3+ tuning AIE self-assembly of CuNCs for sensitively sensing trace fluoride ions [14].

Metal nanoclusters (MNCs), which consist of several to hundreds of metal atoms, have great potential as novel functional materials [15]. They are usually<3 nm in size [16]. Ultra-small size metal clusters exhibit quantum confinement effects and local environment-dependent photoluminescence properties [17,18]. Fluorescent sensors designed with metal nanoclusters have attracted special attention due to their excellent properties such as ultra-small size, excellent optical properties and good biocompatibility. However, most research has focused on the synthesis and application of single metal nanoclusters. In recent years, some fluorescent bimetallic nanocomposites with excellent chemical and physical properties have been prepared [19,20]. Compared with monometallic nanoclusters, bimetallic nanoclusters have higher stability and stronger fluorescence intensity because they can combine the advantages of both nanoclusters [21].

Herein, we developed fluorescent bimetallic gold-copper nanoclusters (AuCuNCs) as a novel and facile fluorescent probe for the sensitive detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- double ions (Scheme 1 ). AuCuNCs nanoprobes with good water solubility and stability were prepared by using green small molecule sodium ascorbate (SA) as reducing agent. The synthesized AuCuNCs exhibited bright blue color under 365 nm UV lamp. When Cr3+ or S2O8 2- was added to the solution of AuCuNCs with stable fluorescence properties, AuCuNCs underwent a significant and selective fluorescence quenching phenomenon. In the experiment, the masking agent EDTA of Cr3+ was added. When Cr3+ left the surface of AuCuNCs and formed metal chelates with EDTA, the fluorescence intensity of AuCuNCs could be restored to a certain extent. In addition, the AuCuNCs nanoprobes were also successfully used to detect Cr3+ and S2O8 2- in lake water and tap water.

Hydrogen Tetrachloroaurate(III) Trihydrate (HAuCl4·3H2O), Copper sulphate (CuSO4), Chromium(III) chloride hexahydrate (CrCl3·6H2O), Sodium persulfate (Na2S2O8), Ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid tetrasodium salt hydrate (EDTA) and other metal salts were purchased from Shanghai Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., LTD. Sodium ascorbate (SA) was purchased from Shanghai Bailingway Technology Co., LTD. All reagents were analytically pure. Ultrapure water (18.3 MΩ·cm) from Millipore Milli-Q instrument was used for solution preparation.

The fluorescence spectroscopy (FL) detections were achieved by a Shimadzu F-7000 fluorescence spectrophotometer (Japan). Ultraviolet–visible (UV–Vis) absorption results were acquired using Shimadzu UV2450 spectrophotometer (Japan). The transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images were obtained by JEOL (JEM-2010, Japan) TEM. Fluorescence lifetime were obtained by using FL-TCSPC state/transient fluorescence lifetime spectrometer (Horiba Jobin Yvon Inc, France). AuCuNCs were visualized by three - purpose ultraviolet instrument (ZF-2, Shanghai).

The AuCuNCs were synthesized by a green and facile method. 100 μL of HAuCl4 (10 mM) was added to 100 μL of Cu(NO3)2 (10 mM). After mixing the solution, stir it at a speed of 1400 r/min and add 100 μL SA (10 mM) solution drop by drop. At the same time, it can be observed that the mixed solution quickly turns black. The AuCuNCs were formed by stirring in a dark room for 24 h until the solution turns clarified.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Determination of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions by AuCuNCs
ce:section.ce:para: All fluorescence measurements were made under the same conditions: excitation and emission slit widths were set to 5 nm and 5 nm, measurement voltage was set to 700 V. The experiment was repeated two to three times for each sample. The raw FL intensity was first measured by adding 190 μL of ultrapure water to 10 μL of AuCuNCs solution. This AuCuNCs nanoprobes were prepared by taking 10 μL of AuCuNCs, adding 10 μL of Cr3+ or S2O8 2- solution at different concentrations, and then adding 180 μL of ultrapure water to ensure a total volume of 200 μL. To test the selectivity of AuCuNCs, we added a series of ions (S2-, SO4 2-, NO3 –, HSO3 -, K+, Na+, Mg2+, SO3 2-, CH3COO–, P2O7 4-, PO4 3-, Cd2+, Cl-, HCO3 –, MoO4 2- ClCH2COO-, CO3 –, Pb2+, NO3 –, Br -, Mn2+, Ni2+, Cr3+ and S2O8 2-) to AuCuNCs solution and reacted at room temperature for 4 h. The change in FL intensity of the above process was then recorded.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence detection of AuCuNCs nanoprobes in real samples
ce:section.ce:para: To test the reliability and practicality of this test method, we examined Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions in tap water and lake water using the standard addition method. These water samples were centrifuged at 1400 r/min for 5 min for pre-treatment. Then, 10 μL of Cr3+ solution (50 μM, 100 mΜ) was added to 10 μL of AuCuNCs, and placed in 180 μL of actual water sample to measure FL intensity. Similarly, 10 μL of S2O8 2- solution (5 μM, 20 mΜ) was added to 10 μL of AuCuNCs, and placed in 180 μL of actual water sample to measure its FL intensity.

TEM was used to characterize the morphology of AuCuNCs. As shown in Fig. 1 A, the prepared AuCuNCs has excellent monodispersity and relatively homogeneous particles. According to the particle size distribution in Fig. 1B, the average size of AuCuNCs is 5.5 ± 0.24 nm. The composition of AuCuNCs was analyzed by energy dispersion X-ray spectroscopy (EDX), and a blank control group was set up to exclude the influence of carrier copper network. Fig. 1C shows that the prepared material contains high content of Au and Cu elements. In Fig. 1D, it is shown as the control group with only Cu element, which is mainly caused by the influence of carbon supporting membrane. The above experiments can prove that AuCuNCs have been successfully prepared.

Blue-emitting AuCuNCs were prepared by reducing HAuCl4 solution and Cu(NO3)2 solution with SA as reducing agent under intense agitation at room temperature. FL spectroscopy was used to obtain the optical properties of AuCuNCs, as shown in Fig. 2 A. AuCuNCs also possess excitation-dependent fluorescence behavior like most fluorescent nanoparticles [22]. When the excitation wavelength gradually increases from 300 nm to 390 nm, the FL maximum emission peak shifts toward the long-wave direction and the FL intensity increases. When the excitation wavelength is 370 nm, AuCuNCs shows the strongest FL emission peak at 455 nm, so the optimal excitation wavelength of AuCuNCs prepared by this method is 370 nm. The ultraviolet spectrum of AuCuNCs solution is shown in Fig. 2B. There is a weak ultraviolet absorption peak at 370 nm, which is the optimal excitation wavelength of AuCuNCs. As shown in the inset of Fig. 2B, AuCuNCs is a light yellow clear and transparent liquid under sunlight (on the left side of the inset), and AuCuNCs shows bright blue under ultraviolet light (on the right side of the inset).

To determine the stability of AuCuNCs under highly saline environment, their FL intensity was measured in the presence of different concentrations of NaCl. As shown in Fig. S1A, only slight changes in the FL intensity of AuCuNCs were observed in the experiments, indicating the high stability of AuCuNCs even in a high salt environment. As shown in Fig. S1B, the FL intensity of AuCuNCs was observed to be strong and stable when the pH range was from 3 to 9, indicating that AuCuNCs can be stable at different pH environmental. These findings suggest that AuCuNCs have the potential for application in ion detection. Finally, AuCuNCs also appeared outstanding optical stability. In Fig. S1C, the FL intensity of AuCuNCs did not change significantly under 90 min continuous scanning.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Determination of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions by AuCuNCs
ce:section.ce:para: We first carried out a feasibility analysis of the experiment. In the absence of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions, AuCuNCs had a strong FL detection peak at around 455 nm (Fig. 3 , a curve). As a comparison, the FL intensity of AuCuNCs underwent a great decrease after the addition of 25 mM Cr3+ and 0.25 mM S2O8 2- ions, respectively. This indicated that Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions can effectively suppress the FL of AuCuNCs (Fig. 3, c and d curves). If the masking agent EDTA of Cr3+ was added, Cr3+ leaves the surface of AuCuNCs and forms metal chelates with EDTA, so that the FL intensity of AuCuNCs was recovered to certain extent (Fig. 3, b curve). The inset corresponding to the serial number in the figure also undergoes a corresponding color change, from bright blue to dark blue under the UV lamp. Therefore, this experiment was worth exploring.

A fluorescent nanoprobe for the detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions was developed based on ion-induced FL quenching of AuCuNCs. In order to optimize the experimental conditions, the kinetics of FL quenching of AuCuNCs after adding Cr3+ or S2O8 2- was discussed. In Figure S2, when Cr3+ reacted with AuCuNCs for about 1 h, the FL no longer changed. Therefore, 3600 s (1 h) was chosen as the optimized time for the quench reaction. Similarly, after the reaction of S2O8 2- with AuCuNCs for about 11520 s (3.2 h), the FL intensity reached stability, so its reaction time was set at 3.2 h.

Further analysis was performed for both Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions. The sensitivity of the AuCuNCs nanoprobes were evaluated by measuring the change in fluorescence with the addition of different concentrations of Cr3+. In Fig. 4 A, when the concentration of Cr3+ increased, the FL intensity of AuCuNCs gradually decreased. And after the Cr3+ concentration increased to 50 mM, the FL intensity of AuCuNCs no longer decreased and was basically completely quenched. Linear analysis was shown in Fig. 4B. When Cr3+ concentration was in the range of 0–4000 μM, the (F0 - F) / F0 value of AuCuNCs showed a good linear response to Cr3+ concentration. The linear equation was y = 0.07475x + 0.01971(R2 = 0.9854, n = 8). The LOD for Cr3+ was 1.5 μM, calculated by the equation LOD = 3σ/k, where σ denotes the standard deviation of the blank measurement and k denotes the slope of the calibration curve. The effect of S2O8 2- concentration on the FL response of AuCuNCs nanoprobes was investigated. When the concentration of S2O8 2- increased gradually, the FL intensity of AuCuNCs decreased gradually. And when the concentration of S2O8 2- increased to 1 mM, the FL intensity of AuCuNCs did not decrease anymore and was basically completely quenched (Fig. 4C). Linear analysis was shown in Fig. 4D, when the concentration of S2O8 2- was in the range of 0–40 μM, the (F0 - F) / F0 value of AuCuNCs showed a good linear response to the concentration of S2O8 2-, the linear equation was y = 0.01073 × - 0.00401(R2 = 0.9893, n = 8), and the LOD was 0.037 μM by calculating the 3σ/K formula. Therefore, AuCuNCs can be used as a fluorescent probe for sensitive detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2-. F is the FL intensity of AuCuNCs after adding Cr3+ or S2O8 2- ions. F0 is the FL intensity of blank AuCuNCs.

Table 1 summarized the results of AuCuNCs nanoprobes detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions and the comparison with other detection methods. Obviously, the proposed method has a lower LOD and a wider detection range than other sensing materials.

Fluorescence lifetime is commonly used to evaluate the type of fluorescence quenching. Dynamic quenching refers to the interaction between a quencher and an excited state molecule of the fluorophore leading to the decrease of FL intensity. Static quenching refers to the formation of a non-fluorescent ground-state complex through the coordination of a quencher and a ground state molecule of the fluorophore to cause decrease of FL intensity [33]. In static quenching, if the concentration of the quencher increases, the fluorescence lifetime remains unchanged because any remaining fluorophore fraction that does not complex with the quencher retains its original fluorescence lifetime. In contrast, increasing the concentration of the quencher in dynamic quenching decreases the fluorescence lifetime [34,35].

We performed fluorescence lifetime measurements to investigate the luminescence properties of AuCuNCs in the presence of mixed Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions (Fig. 5 ). Combined with the fluorescence lifetime decay curve and fitting results, it was obvious to observe the change of fluorescence lifetime of the three substances. As shown in Table S1, the lifetimes of the three samples were 2.84 μs, 3.48 μs and 1.98 μs, respectively. The fluorescence lifetime of AuCuNCs decreased after adding 5 mM Cr3+, so this quenching process may be caused by dynamic quenching. The fluorescence lifetime of AuCuNCs increased after adding 0.5 mM S2O8 2-, and this quenching process may be caused by static quenching.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Response of AuCuNCs to different anions and cations
ce:section.ce:para: To investigate the sensing selectivity of the AuCuNCs probes detect Cr3+, the FL response of the probes were studied in the presence of other different interfering ions with a final concentration of 20 mM, such as Cd2+、K+、Mg2+、Pb2+、Mn2+、Ni2+、PO4 3−、S2−、SO3 2−、Cl−、CO3 2−、HCO3 −、NO3 −、P2O7 4-、SO4 2-、CH3COO−、CH2ClCOO−、Br−、HSO3 −. The FL intensity of AuCuNCs was observed, and the results were shown in Fig. S3A. The (F0 - F) / F0 was took as the evaluation index, the value of (F0 - F) / F0 did not change obviously when interfering ions were added. Only when Cr3+ was added, the value of (F0 - F) / F0 increased significantly. Similarly, selective experiments were carried out for detecting S2O8 2-. When Cd2+、K+、Mg2+、Pb2+、Mn2+、Ni2+、PO4 3−、MoO4 2-、S2−、SO3 2−、Cl−、CO3 2−、HCO3 −、NO3 −、P2O7 4-、SO4 2-、CH3COO−、CH2ClCOO−、Br− and HSO3 − (the final concentration of all ions was 50 μM) were added as interference ions, the results were shown in Fig. S3B. Only when adding S2O8 2-, the value of (F0 - F) / F0 increased significantly. Obviously, the (F0 - F) / F0 was significantly affected by Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions, which indicated that AuCuNCs had the ability to selectively detect Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions. Under UV irradiation, the presence of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions quenched the blue luminescence of AuCuNCs, while other ions kept blue light (The inset in Figure S3). These phenomenon were consistent with the FL intensity changes of AuCuNCs probes.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions in real samples
ce:section.ce:para: The Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions in lake water and tap water were evaluated using the standard addition method to examine the working performance of the sensors in real samples. The specific procedure is that when a certain concentration of Cr3+ or S2O8 2- ions was added to the lake water and tap water samples, their signals are measured by fluorescence spectrometry. Their recovery values can be calculated from the corresponding linear equation. The actual calculation results were shown in Table S2. The recovery rates of Cr3+ ranged from 96.66 % to 116.29 % with RSD (Relative standard deviation) < 5.5 %, and the recovery rates of S2O8 2- ranged from 95.75 % to 119.4 % with RSD < 6.9 %, which indicated that the AuCuNCs fluorescence sensors has good applicability and can be used for actual sample analysis.

In conclusion, the blue-emitting bimetallic AuCuNCs were synthesized by a green and facile method. The prepared AuCuNCs displayed well-monodispersed near-spherical particles with average size of 5.5 nm, and showed good water solubility, excellent photostability and biocompatibility. Meanwhile, using the bimetallic AuCuNCs as the fluorescence nanoprobe, the “turn off” fluorescence detection of Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions can be carried out. Furthermore, the AuCuNCs nanoprobes has high selectivity and sensitivity for detecting Cr3+ and S2O8 2- ions in real water. This experiment not only expands the green synthesis method of nanoclusters, but also opens the application prospect of nanoclusters in environmental protection and water quality monitoring.

Zhiying Shi: Formal analysis, Writing – original draft, Investigation. Bangyang Hu: Data curation, Validation. Shengya Ge: Investigation, Validation. Baozhu Chi: Funding acquisition, Project administration. Xiluan Yan: Funding acquisition, Project administration. Xiangjuan Zheng: Conceptualization, Supervision, Methodology, Resources, Writing – review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was financially supported by the Natural Science Foundation of Jiangxi Province 20212BAB206093 and National Natural Science Foundation of China (No.22064012, 21907048, 31960207 and 81860610).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.saa.2023.122855.

ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
